metadata.title: City Nickname Generator for Writers, RPGs, and Worldbuilding
metadata.description: Generate city nicknames for fantasy, noir, sci-fi, and modern settings. Create official, local, legendary, or mocking city epithets for worldbuilding.
metadata.keywords: city nickname generator, fictional city nickname generator, fantasy city nickname generator, city nickname ideas, town nickname generator, city epithet generator, worldbuilding city nickname

ui.title: City Nickname Generator
ui.subtitle: Generate city nicknames for fictional worlds, RPG settings, and story locations. Create official titles, local nicknames, legendary epithets, or mocking slang in seconds.
ui.city_type_placeholder: e.g. port city, capital, industrial city, fortress town
ui.reputation_placeholder: e.g. wealthy, corrupt, sacred, lawless, forgotten
ui.known_for_placeholder: e.g. neon markets and smugglers, gold domes and royal processions
ui.geography_placeholder: e.g. coastal, high mountain pass, underground
ui.empty_output: Fill in your city details and generate to see grouped nickname ideas here.

validation.city_type_required: Choose what kind of city this is.
validation.known_for_required: Tell us what the city is known for.
validation.style_required: Choose at least one nickname style.
validation.generic_error: We couldn't generate city nicknames right now. Please try again.

random_prompts.knownFor: gold domes, royal processions, ancient temples

feature1.name: feature_intro
feature1.label: Overview
feature1.title: What This City Nickname Generator Is Good At
feature1.description: Built for worldbuilders, RPG game masters, and fiction writers who need believable city names that carry weight. Instead of flat lists, you get grouped nickname categories with short explanations and genre-aware context.

items.title: Four nickname perspectives
items.description: Official, local, legendary, and mocking nickname groups reveal how different factions and social classes refer to the same city.

items.title: Explanations that fit your world
items.description: Every nickname includes a 'why it fits' note and a 'best for' use case so you understand what the name implies before you use it.

items.title: Genre and tone aware
items.description: From dark fantasy to cyberpunk to noir, every genre and tone produces distinct nickname language that matches your setting's vocabulary.

how_to_use.name: how_to_use
how_to_use.label: HOW TO USE
how_to_use.title: How to Use the City Nickname Generator in 3 Steps
how_to_use.description: The city nickname generator takes you from city concept to believable nickname in under a minute.

items.title: Describe your city
items.description: Enter the city type, what it's known for, genre, tone, and reputation. Add geography or ruling power for more specific results.

items.title: Choose nickname styles
items.description: Select which nickname perspectives you want — official titles, local slang, legendary epithets, or mocking insults.

items.title: Copy your favorites
items.description: Browse grouped results, read why each nickname fits, and copy the ones that match your story or campaign.

feature2.name: feature_benefits
feature2.label: Benefits
feature2.title: Why Writers and Game Masters Use the City Nickname Generator
feature2.description: Good world names do more than sound cool — they carry social meaning, history, and faction identity.

items.title: Names that carry faction weight
items.description: Official names reflect ruling power. Local nicknames show how residents cope. Mocking names reveal class tension. Each group adds a layer of world depth.

items.title: Short explanations, immediate use
items.description: The 'why it fits' note answers the question your editor or player will ask — so you can use the name confidently instead of second-guessing it.

items.title: Works across every genre
items.description: Fantasy kingdoms, noir cities, cyberpunk sprawls, and post-apocalyptic settlements all have distinct nickname vocabularies — the city nickname generator matches each one.

feature3.name: use_cases
feature3.label: Use Cases
feature3.title: Who Uses the City Nickname Generator
feature3.description: The city nickname generator helps wherever a location needs to feel like a real place with a history.

items.title: RPG game masters
items.description: Give players a city that locals call something different than the map label. The nickname table, the tavern slang, the ominous legend — all three at once.

items.title: Fiction and worldbuilding writers
items.description: Drop a local nickname into dialogue and a city suddenly has street-level texture. No worldbuilding document needed — readers feel the history.

items.title: Game designers and narrative teams
items.description: Prototype location naming fast across different city archetypes. Generate, filter by style, and hand off shortlists to the writing team.

items.title: What is a city nickname in worldbuilding?
items.description: A city nickname is an informal or formal epithet that gives a settlement a reputation-driven identity beyond its official name. 'The City That Never Sleeps' or 'The Iron Capital' are real-world examples. In fiction, these names signal faction identity, class tension, and history without exposition.

items.title: What do the four nickname styles mean?
items.description: Official nicknames are titles used in formal documents, by nobility, or in official propaganda. Local nicknames are what residents actually say on the street. Legendary nicknames come from myth, prophecy, or ancient events. Mocking nicknames are used by outsiders, rivals, or lower classes to undercut the city's pretensions.

items.title: Can I use city nickname generator results in published work?
items.description: Yes. The generated nicknames are yours to use in fiction, games, and any commercial or non-commercial creative work. There is no attribution requirement.

items.title: Does the city nickname generator work for real cities too?
items.description: The city nickname generator is tuned for fictional cities and worldbuilding contexts. It works best when you describe a setting, genre, and tone rather than a real-world location.

items.title: How do I get more specific results?
items.description: Fill in the optional fields — geography, ruling power or culture, and city name. The more context you give, the more the nicknames reflect actual details of your world instead of genre defaults.

related_tools.title: More tools for worldbuilders
related_tools.description: Keep shaping your world — titles, backstories, and outlines for the cities you just named.
